title: Antirrhinum glutinosum
taxonomy: Antirrhinum glutinosum is classified within the plant kingdom. It belongs to the family Plantaginaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the plantain or lopseed family. Antirrhinum glutinosum is further classified in the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This species is a part of the genus Antirrhinum, which consists of several species of snapdragons. Its classification also includes the rank of species, making it a distinct entity within the larger plant kingdom.
uses: Antirrhinum glutinosum is traditionally used in folk medicine for its supposed antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been employed to treat various conditions, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used in herbal remedies. Antirrhinum glutinosum is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.

intro: Antirrhinum glutinosum, commonly known as sticky snapdragon, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region. The health benefits of Antirrhinum glutinosum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Antirrhinum glutinosum is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat coughs, colds, and bronchitis.
